---
title: "PSI Conference 2026 Presentation Code"
description: "Fast, Fresh & Interactive: R Dashboards for Statisticians in 30 Minutes or Less!"
author:
  - name: PSI AIMS
    url: https://psiaims.github.io/website/
date: 18-05-2026
categories: [Conference]
draft: false
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-copy: true
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
#| include: false
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(DT)
library(htmlwidgets)
library(pharmaverseadamjnj)

adsl <- pharmaverseadamjnj::adsl

adsl2 <- adsl %>%
  select(USUBJID, TRT01P, TRTDURD, WEIGHTBL, HEIGHTBL) %>%
  filter(!is.na(TRT01P) & !is.na(TRTDURD))

adsl3 <- adsl2 %>%
  mutate_if(is.character, as.factor)

tf_colors <- c(
  "Placebo" = "#0072B2",
  "Xanomeline High Dose" = "#D55E00",
  "Xanomeline Low Dose" = "#CC79A7"
)

f1 <- ggplot(data = adsl3, aes(x = TRT01P, y = TRTDURD, fill = TRT01P)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Treatment Group") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 250),
    breaks = seq(0, 250, 50),
    expand = c(0.05, 0.05),
    name = "Treatment Duration (Days)"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = tf_colors,
    name = "Treatment Group"
  )

g1 <- ggplotly(f1)
saveWidget(g1, "boxplotly.html")

f2 <- ggplot(data = adsl3, aes(x = HEIGHTBL, y = WEIGHTBL, shape = TRT01P, colour = TRT01P)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 150),
    breaks = seq(0, 150, 10),
    name = "Baseline Height (cm)"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 150),
    breaks = seq(0, 150, 10),
    name = "Baseline Weight (kg)"
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c(16, 17, 15),
    name = "Treatment Group"
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = tf_colors,
    name = "Treatment Group"
  )

g2 <- ggplotly(f2)
saveWidget(g2, "scatterplotly.html")

my_table <- adsl3

my_table_2 <- my_table %>%
  rename(
    "Unique Subject Identifier" = USUBJID,
    "Treatment Group" = TRT01P,
    "Total Treatment Duration (Days)" = TRTDURD,
    "Baseline Weight (kg)" = WEIGHTBL,
    "Baseline Height (cm)" = HEIGHTBL
  )

d <- datatable(
  my_table_2,
  extensions = c("ColReorder"),
  rownames = FALSE,
  filter = "top",
  class = "stripe hover compact",
  options = list(
    colReorder = TRUE,
    pageLength = 5,
    lengthMenu = c(5, 10, 12, 20, 50, 100)
  )
)
saveWidget(d, "datatable.html")
```

## Setup

```r
install.packages("tibble", type = "binary")
install.packages("dplyr", type = "binary")
install.packages("ggplot2", type = "binary")
install.packages("plotly", type = "binary")
install.packages("DT", type = "binary")
install.packages("htmlwidgets", type = "binary")
install.packages("pharmaverseadamjnj")
```

```r
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(DT)
library(htmlwidgets)
library(pharmaverseadamjnj)
```

```r
adsl <- pharmaverseadamjnj::adsl

adsl2 <- adsl %>%
  select(USUBJID, TRT01P, TRTDURD, WEIGHTBL, HEIGHTBL) %>%
  filter(!is.na(TRT01P) & !is.na(TRTDURD))

adsl3 <- adsl2 %>%
  mutate_if(is.character, as.factor)

tf_colors <- c(
  "Placebo" = "#0072B2",
  "Xanomeline High Dose" = "#D55E00",
  "Xanomeline Low Dose" = "#CC79A7"
)
```

## Recipe 1: Make A Figure Interactive

### Boxplot: Create The Static Figure

```r
f1 <- ggplot(data = adsl3, aes(x = TRT01P, y = TRTDURD, fill = TRT01P)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Treatment Group") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 250),
    breaks = seq(0, 250, 50),
    expand = c(0.05, 0.05),
    name = "Treatment Duration (Days)"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = tf_colors,
    name = "Treatment Group"
  )
```

```{r}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 4.8
f1
```

### Boxplot: Make It Interactive

```r
g1 <- ggplotly(f1)

saveWidget(g1, "boxplotly.html")
```

```{r}
g1
```

[Open the interactive boxplot](boxplotly.html)

### Scatterplot: Create The Static Figure

```r
f2 <- ggplot(data = adsl3, aes(x = HEIGHTBL, y = WEIGHTBL, shape = TRT01P, colour = TRT01P)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 150),
    breaks = seq(0, 150, 10),
    name = "Baseline Height (cm)"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 150),
    breaks = seq(0, 150, 10),
    name = "Baseline Weight (kg)"
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c(16, 17, 15),
    name = "Treatment Group"
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = tf_colors,
    name = "Treatment Group"
  )
```

```{r}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 4.8
f2
```

### Scatterplot: Make It Interactive

```r
g2 <- ggplotly(f2)

saveWidget(g2, "scatterplotly.html")
```

```{r}
g2
```

[Open the interactive scatterplot](scatterplotly.html)

## Recipe 2: Make A Table Explorable

### Prepare The Table

```r
my_table <- adsl3

my_table_2 <- my_table %>%
  rename(
    "Unique Subject Identifier" = USUBJID,
    "Treatment Group" = TRT01P,
    "Total Treatment Duration (Days)" = TRTDURD,
    "Baseline Weight (kg)" = WEIGHTBL,
    "Baseline Height (cm)" = HEIGHTBL
  )
```

### Make It Interactive

```r
d <- datatable(
  my_table_2,
  extensions = c("ColReorder"),
  rownames = FALSE,
  filter = "top",
  class = "stripe hover compact",
  options = list(
    colReorder = TRUE,
    pageLength = 5,
    lengthMenu = c(5, 10, 12, 20, 50, 100)
  )
)

saveWidget(d, "datatable.html")
```

```{r}
d
```

[Open the interactive table](datatable.html)

## Recipe 3: Bring Outputs Together

[Open the Quarto dashboard](PSI%20Conf%202026%20Dashboard.html)

## Full Code

```r
# PSI Conf 2026 interactivity recipes

install.packages("tibble", type = "binary")
install.packages("dplyr", type = "binary")
install.packages("ggplot2", type = "binary")
install.packages("plotly", type = "binary")
install.packages("DT", type = "binary")
install.packages("htmlwidgets", type = "binary")
install.packages("pharmaverseadamjnj")

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(DT)
library(htmlwidgets)
library(pharmaverseadamjnj)


# Data --------------------------------------------------------------------

adsl <- pharmaverseadamjnj::adsl

adsl2 <- adsl %>%
  select(USUBJID, TRT01P, TRTDURD, WEIGHTBL, HEIGHTBL) %>%
  filter(!is.na(TRT01P) & !is.na(TRTDURD))

adsl3 <- adsl2 %>%
  mutate_if(is.character, as.factor)

tf_colors <- c(
  "Placebo" = "#0072B2",
  "Xanomeline High Dose" = "#D55E00",
  "Xanomeline Low Dose" = "#CC79A7"
)


# Recipe 1: Make a figure interactive ------------------------------------

f1 <- ggplot(data = adsl3, aes(x = TRT01P, y = TRTDURD, fill = TRT01P)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Treatment Group") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 250),
    breaks = seq(0, 250, 50),
    expand = c(0.05, 0.05),
    name = "Treatment Duration (Days)"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = tf_colors,
    name = "Treatment Group"
  )

g1 <- ggplotly(f1)

saveWidget(g1, "boxplotly.html")


f2 <- ggplot(data = adsl3, aes(x = HEIGHTBL, y = WEIGHTBL, shape = TRT01P, colour = TRT01P)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 150),
    breaks = seq(0, 150, 10),
    name = "Baseline Height (cm)"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 150),
    breaks = seq(0, 150, 10),
    name = "Baseline Weight (kg)"
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c(16, 17, 15),
    name = "Treatment Group"
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = tf_colors,
    name = "Treatment Group"
  )

g2 <- ggplotly(f2)

saveWidget(g2, "scatterplotly.html")


# Recipe 2: Make a table explorable --------------------------------------

my_table <- adsl3

my_table_2 <- my_table %>%
  rename(
    "Unique Subject Identifier" = USUBJID,
    "Treatment Group" = TRT01P,
    "Total Treatment Duration (Days)" = TRTDURD,
    "Baseline Weight (kg)" = WEIGHTBL,
    "Baseline Height (cm)" = HEIGHTBL
  )

d <- datatable(
  my_table_2,
  extensions = c("ColReorder"),
  rownames = FALSE,
  filter = "top",
  class = "stripe hover compact",
  options = list(
    colReorder = TRUE,
    pageLength = 5,
    lengthMenu = c(5, 10, 12, 20, 50, 100)
  )
)

saveWidget(d, "datatable.html")
```
